data.title: Super Sales India to Acquire 26% Stake in Indian Solar Power Company
data.description:
Gears manufacturer Super Sales India has agreed to acquire a 26% stake in solar power company Aghanaswath Helios Energy for 260,000 Indian rupees, according to a Wednesday filing to the BSE.

The acquisition is aimed at purchasing solar power for the company's use under the group captive concept.

The transaction is expected to be completed by the end of April.

Shares of the company were up nearly 2% in recent trade.

data.title: What the Fed could do as stock-market rout spreads to bonds, bank lending
data.description:
By Steve Goldstein

Emergency rate cut and temporary purchases of bonds are alternatives

The Federal Reserve wanted to sit back and monitor how an economy where inflation already was elevated would respond to sweeping tariffs implemented by the Trump administration.

But financial-market gyrations may not give it the time to wait. The yield on the 10-year Treasury BX:TMUBMUSD10Y spiked as high as 4.52% as midnight struck, with Wednesday bringing the highest tariffs in a century into effect.

That's a swift reversal from yields that were just 4% last week, though news that China wanted to have dialogue with the U.S. helped settle some nerves early Wednesday.

Markets are now giving credence to the idea the Fed could make an emergency rate-cut. Based on the April federal funds futures contract, the market was pricing in a roughly one-in-five chance of a reduction before the central bank's next scheduled meeting.

There are other alternatives as well. When the U.K. bond market tumbled after a negative reaction to Liz Truss's mini-budget in 2022, the Bank of England intervened with a temporary purchase of longer-dated government debt - precisely the segment of the U.S. bond market under threat.

It was an intervention that was successful - stemming the bond-market turmoil that had seen 30-year gilt yields BX:TMBMKGB-30Y surge 130 basis points in just three days, even making a profit of GBP3.5 billion on the GBP19.3 billion of purchases it made, and then unwound.

TS Lombard strategist Dario Perkins pointed out that after the intervention, the Bank of England resumed hiking interest rates, lifting them by another 300 basis points as it fought surging inflation. It even resumed its quantitative-tightening plan of reducing the bonds on its balance sheet.

"You can intervene briefly in bonds and stay hawkish on inflation," says Perkins.

The Fed could be even more technical, focusing on the overnight borrowing market and what's called the Secured Overnight Financing Rate.

There, the swap rate between SOFR and the equivalent maturity Treasury are tumbling - which suggests liquidity is vanishing and that banks aren't lending to each other.

-Steve Goldstein

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: China Seeks to Build Ties With Asian Neighbors During Trade War
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

China's top leaders emerged from a two-day huddle to say they want more trade and cooperation with Asia.

Leader Xi Jinping and Communist Party elites met Tuesday and Wednesday to discuss relations with neighboring countries, said state news agency Xinhua. It said top officials wanted deeper economic integration with neighbors and more cooperation on supply chains. Economists have said China is likely to look to increase exports to Asia and Europe now that exports to the U.S. are poised to collapse.

Xinhua's report didn't mention the U.S. or President Trump.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Bang & Olufsen to Increase Prices in Response to TariffsUpdate
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

Bang & Olufsen will soon increase prices in response to U.S. President Trump's import tariffs, it said Wednesday.

The Danish consumer-electronics company implemented modest price increases on selected products in January, but further price increases will be made by May 1 partly due to the recent tariffs.

Bang & Olufsen products are made in Denmark and exported, so the company is exposed to the new 20% tariff on EU goods imported into the U.S. The company doesn't break down its revenue by individual market, but the Americas region in general accounts for around 14% of group revenue.

The company said that despite the tariffs it will continue investing in its strategy to strengthen its market position. Late last year it raised 228 million Danish kroner ($33.5 million) through a share issue, with funds earmarked for its strategy that aims to strengthen its position in the luxury audio market by increasing brand awareness, optimizing its retail network and investing in new products.

"Amid geopolitical uncertainties including the recent announced tariffs and possibility of further tariff changes, we are continuing our strategic transition by investing the proceeds of our recent capital raise in future profitable growth," Chief Executive Kristian Tear said.

Tear said the strategic transition continued according to plan in its fiscal third quarter ended Feb. 28 with revenue growth driven by most markets in EMEA and a strong performance in the Americas. Revenue in the Asia-Pacific region declined, mainly due to a sharp decline in China.

The company added that its guidance for the fiscal year ahead remains unchanged, but pointed to uncertainty related to consumer sentiment and the continued geopolitical and economic uncertainty.

It still expects revenue growth in local currencies of between minus 3% to positive 3%, with an earnings before interest and taxes margin before special items of minus 2% to positive 1%. Free cash flow is expected between zero and minus 100 million kroner.

However, it now expects revenue growth to be in the lower end of the range due to persistent challenges in Asia-Pacific.

The pre-items EBIT margin is expected to be in the mid-range mainly due to the recent positive development of its gross margin, while free cash flow is expected to be in the higher end of the range.

The guidance assumes no significant impact from tariffs for the remainder of the year.

Bang & Olufsen posted a net profit of 7 million kroner in its third quarter as revenue rose 2.8% to 631 million kroner.

Analysts had expected a net profit of 2 million kroner on revenue of 649 million kroner, according to a FactSet poll.

The gross margin rose to 55.4% in the quarter from 53.2% in the same period a year prior.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: Accor, InterGlobe Partner to Build Network of 300 Hotels in India
data.description:
By Pierre Bertrand

France's Accor and Indian travel conglomerate InterGlobe Enterprises aim to build a network of 300 hotels in India over the next five years as they seek to create the country's fastest-growing hospitality company.

The companies said they would bring together their currently owned assets, development and management businesses in the country in a bid to expand in India's hospitality market.

"This new entity will become the exclusive vehicle for growing all Accor brands in India, including luxury and lifestyle brands from Ennismore, Accor's fast-growing hospitality portfolio," the companies said Wednesday.

Hospitality company Accor will continue to lead operations, brand management and provide full access to its brands and services, they said.

As part of the partnership, the companies said they would jointly invest in and become the largest shareholder in Treebo, an Indian company that manages 800 hotels across 120 cities.

In addition, the companies said that Treebo would lead the development of the Ibis and Mercure brands in India through a license agreement.

The companies said that the combined portfolio of Accor and Treebo would create India's third-largest hospitality player, with more than 30,000 rooms.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: UK 30-year gilt yields surge to highest since 1998
data.description:
British 30-year government bond yields surged to their highest since 1998 on Wednesday, catching up with a sharp overnight rise in 30-year U.S. Treasury yields driven by U.S. President Donald Trump hitting China with 104% tariffs.

The 30-year British gilt yield (GB30YT=RR) hit 5.507% at 0713 GMT, up 16 basis points on the day and pushing past a previous multi-decade high of 5.472% set in January.

On Monday 30-year gilt yields rocketed by almost 19 bps, the largest one-day rise since October 2022 in the aftermath of former Prime Minister Liz Truss' failed "mini-budget".

Ten-year gilt yields (GB10YT=RR) were 8 basis points higher at 4.69% at 0726 GMT, while two-year yields (GB2YT=RR) were down 9 bps at 3.87% as investors stepped up bets that the Bank of England would have to cut rates faster this year.

Interest rate futures price in 85 basis points in rate cuts by the BoE this year and see a rate cut of at least a quarter point on May 8 after the BoE's next meeting as a near certainty, with a roughly 10% chance of a bigger half-point move.

Before Trump detailed his tariff plans last week, markets saw only a 50% chance of a rate cut next month and just over 50 bps of loosening between now and the end of 2025.

Finance minister Rachel Reeves pushed back on Tuesday against suggestions that Trump's tariffs would require her to suspend fiscal rules aimed at achieving a balanced current budget by 2029/30.

But in a fiscal update last month she had only a small amount of leeway to hit this goal which could easily be erased by higher borrowing costs or weaker growth.

"Higher yields are going to make it difficult politically to decide what to do next," said Michael Metcalfe, head of macro strategy at State Street Global Markets in London.

"If yields go up and stay up this causes problem for the UK fiscal position and could put pressure on sterling as well."

data.title: UK's Assura gains on KKR-Stonepeak takeover deal
data.description:
** Shares of British healthcare REIT Assura up 5.4% at 47.7p

** Co agrees to 1.61 billion pounds ($2.06 billion) takeover bid from KKR & Stonepeak Partners

** Under terms of deal, shareholders to get 48.56p in cash for each share held & quarterly interim dividend of 0.84p

** Shares of Primary Health Properties , whose 1.5 billion pounds proposal Assura rejected, opened lower before reversing course to gain 0.4%

** PHP did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment

** As of last close, stock up ~18.42, PHP up ~1.2% YTD

data.title: UK's Petra Diamonds drops as US tariffs lead to potential sales delay
data.description:
** Shares of diamond miner Petra Diamonds down as much as 6.1% at 26.3p

** Stock among top losers in FTSE small-cap index (.FTSC), which is down 1.4%

** Co's Tender 5 sales for Cullinan Mine (approx. 200,000 carats) postponed due to uncertainty over the U.S. tariffs

** Says ongoing market issues, including U.S. tariffs, are affecting pricing assumptions for FY 2025

** Peel Hunt reiterates a "buy" rating with a target price of 50 pence

** YTD stock down ~9.6%

data.title: Kunming Chuan Jin Nuo Chemical Says Not Affected by US Tariffs
data.description:
Kunming Chuan Jin Nuo Chemical said its business operations remain unaffected by the recent US tariff measures.

The chemical manufacturer confirmed it has no import or export business with North America, insulating it from the impact of US reciprocal tariffs.

The company said its main export markets include South Asia, Southeast Asia, and South America.

data.title: Sweco Appoints Former Alfa Laval CFO to Same Role
data.description:
Engineering and architecture consultancy Sweco (SWEC-A.ST, SWEC-B.ST) on Wednesday named Jan Allde its new chief financial officer, effective Aug. 1.

Allde, who will succeed Olof Stålnacke in the role, most recently served as CFO of water and waste treatment company Alfa Laval .

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • NONE

data.title: ONGC, Oil India fall as crude prices hit over 4-year low
data.description:
** Shares of oil explorers Oil and Natural Gas Corporation and Oil India drop 1.5% and 1.7% respectively

** Oil prices fell to their lowest in more than four years on looming demand concerns fuelled by an escalating tariff war between the U.S. and China

** Lower crude prices reduce the average selling price for exploration companies such as ONGC and OILI, weighing on their earnings

** Since U.S. President Donald Trump announced new tariffs on April 2, shares of these two companies have fallen 11% and 11.7% respectively

** Over the same period, Nifty Oil & Gas (.NIFOILGAS) and Nifty Energy fell 5% and 5.4% respectively

data.title: TTF Prices Fall Further to 7-Month Low
data.description:
European natural gas futures fell below €35/MWh, hitting their lowest level since September as President Trump’s tariffs fueled fears of a global economic slowdown and weaker energy demand.

The trade measures have shaken financial markets, with stocks and oil prices tumbling.

For Europe, softer global demand may ease competition for LNG cargoes just as the region begins rebuilding depleted gas reserves after a colder winter.

Storage injections have already picked up, recently exceeding the five-year average.

Meanwhile, the EU is considering more flexible storage rules.

Member states broadly support allowing up to a 10% deviation from the current target of 90% full by November 1, according to reports.

data.title: Housing demand to get a fillip from RBI's second consecutive rate cut, observers say
data.description:
Homebuyers and developers have welcomed the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) announcement to cut its repo rate by 25 basis points on April 9 to 6 percent from 6.25 percent, with observers saying that a second consecutive cut in the policy rate will help drive more sales in the real estate market through lower equated monthly instalments (EMIs) on home loans, especially at a time when some concerns have emerged over slowing home sales in some markets.

While the 25-basis point cut to the repo rate in February helped ease some of the EMI burden that homeowners carried due to high interest rates, the continued high cost of funds for banks and financial institutions mitigated some of the relief. Real estate observers said that the affordable housing segment can be a key beneficiary of the rate cut, with the segment currently going through a period of sluggish sales due to low supply and struggles among entry-level homebuyers.

"Lower interest rates translate into more affordable home loans, which will likely spur housing demand across various categories. This move is expected to lead to higher residential sales, better liquidity in the market and a gradual clearing of unsold inventory—giving developers the confidence to launch new projects. Affordable housing, in particular, stands to gain significantly. With reduced borrowing costs, more aspiring homeowners can enter the market, helping address the pressing demand-supply gap in this segment," said G Hari Babu, national president, National Real Estate Development Council.

Developers can also benefit from the resulting cheaper sources of credit, said observers.

“We welcome the RBI’s decision to reduce the repo rate by 25 basis points to 6.00 percent. At a time of global economic volatility caused by increasing tariffs and geopolitical tensions , this move reflects a continued shift toward a pro-growth stance amid easing inflation and a stabilising macroeconomic outlook. With CPI (Consumer Price Index) inflation expected to moderate to 4.5 percent, the rate cut is well-timed to uplift consumer sentiment and enhance borrowing capacity, especially in the housing sector. It is likely to improve home loan affordability, stimulate housing demand and provide a strong impetus to the mid-income and affordable segments, where interest rate sensitivity remains high. The RBI’s continued policy easing highlights its commitment to sustaining growth while safeguarding macroeconomic stability,” said Boman Irani, managing director, Rustomjee Group, the promoter of listed Keystone Developers, and president of Confederation of Real Estate Developers Associations of India.

Observers also noted the central bank's change in its stance to "accommodative" from "neutral", in the face of a widening global trade war, which he added will help maintain domestic demand and mitigate adverse effects from blanket tariffs imposed by the US.

Others said that newer investment avenues in real estate may also be given a boost by the RBI's second consecutive rate cut."It (the rate cut) provides an encouraging signal for new-age fund and development companies to invest further, strengthening the sector and supporting broader economic growth," said Mohit Malhotra, co-founder and CEO of NeoLiv, a platform that supports fund management and project development.

data.title: European healthcare drops as Trump says tariffs on sector coming soon
data.description:
** Shares in European healthcare companies fall after U.S. President Donald Trump said tariffs for the sector would be announced soon

** Europe's benchmark STOXX 600 Healthcare index (.SXDP) falls 3.9%, leading sectoral losses on the broader STOXX 600 that falls 2.8%

** Among single stocks, Switzerland's Novartis and Roche fall 5.4% and 5.2%, respectively

** France's Sanofi drops 4.9%, Valneva is down 2.9%

** Belgium's Argenx and UCB are down between 4.5% and 5.9%

** Germany's Bayer and Merck fall around 3.5%

** AstraZeneca , Bavarian Nordic and GSK are down 2.2% to 4.7%

data.title: Privi Speciality Chemicals Receives Industrial Promotion Subsidy from Government of Maharashtra, India
data.description:
Privi Speciality Chemicals (BOM:530717) said the Government of Maharashtra, India, has provisionally sanctioned the Industrial Promotion Subsidy for the financial year 2023-24 under the State Incentive Scheme, according to an Indian bourse filing on Tuesday.

The company is eligible for a subsidy of 26.3 million Indian rupees, with 23.7 million rupees to be disbursed immediately upon submission of required documents, the filing said. The remaining 10%, or 2.6 million, will be released after the completion of the GST audit for the financial year 2023-24, it added.

data.description:

data.title: Bulgaria's Sirma shareholders approve share buyback plan
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), April 9 (SeeNews) - Bulgarian software developer Sirma Group Holding [BUL:SGH] said its shareholders endorsed a share repurchase programme targeting up to 5.3 million shares by the end of 2028.

Sirma Group will buy back the shares at a price from 0.85 levs ($0.48/0.43 euro) to 4 levs apiece, with the maximum sum allocated for the set at 21.2 million levs, the company said in a bourse filing on Tuesday.

The initiative, targeting the repurchase of up to 3% of the company's equity capital per year, aims to create a pool of shares for management and employee bonus schemes, Sirma said earlier. The repurchased shares will also serve as a strategic tool for future acquisitions.

Shares in Sirma Group Holding closed at 1.06 levs on the Bulgarian Stock Exchange on Tuesday, bourse data show.

(1 euro = 1.95583 levs)

data.title: French Stocks Track European Peers
data.description:
The CAC 40 plunged 2.3% to 6,935 on Wednesday, tracking European peers and erasing notable gains from the previous session, as the escalating global trade war intensified amid the implementation of US President Donald Trump’s aggressive tariff policies.

These measures, which include a 20% levy on European Union imports, went into effect today.

In retaliation, the European Commission is reportedly considering imposing tariffs of up to 25% on over €22 billion worth of American agricultural and industrial exports.

The majority of stocks traded significantly lower, with Sanofi leading the losses as its shares plunged 5.1%.

STMicroelectronics followed closely, plummeting 5%, while Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield declined 3.8%.

The luxury sector also took a hit, with Kering falling 3.3%, LVMH dropping 3.2%, and Hermes slipping 0.5%.

In addition, financial stocks remained under pressure, with BNP Paribas sliding 3.1%, Societe Generale down 2.9%, Credit Agricole dropping 1.6%, and AXA falling 1.4%.

data.title: Nordex Lands 750-MW Wind Turbine Orders in Türkiye
data.description:
Nordex signed two wind turbine contracts totaling 750 megawatts from Enerjisa Üretim, a joint venture between E.ON and Sabanci Holding.

The agreements cover the delivery of 108 N163/6.X wind turbines at the Edirne and Balkaya wind projects in Türkiye, according to a Wednesday release by the German wind power company.

data.title: Bank of Communications Posts 'Positive' Growth in Q1 Net Profit
data.description:
Bank of Communications achieved "positive" year-on-year growth in both net profit and net interest income in the first quarter, according to a Tuesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The lender's balance of loans increased by more than 360 billion yuan as of the end of March compared with the end of December 2024, the filing said.

data.title: 7-Eleven Owner Posts Higher Quarterly Net Profit on Asset-Sales Gains
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

Seven & i Holdings reported a rise in quarterly net profit as gains from asset sales helped offset weaker earnings from convenience-store operations at home and abroad, underscoring the challenges the 7-Eleven parent faces as it tries to stave off a takeover attempt by the Canadian owner of Circle K.

The Japanese owner of 7-Eleven said Wednesday that net profit more than doubled from a year earlier to 109.44 billion yen, equivalent to $748.1 million, for the three months ended Feb. 28. That beat the estimate of Y98.5 billion in a poll of analysts by data provider Visible Alpha.

Fourth-quarter revenue edged 0.4% higher to Y2.903 trillion.

Operating profit declined for both its domestic and overseas convenience-store businesses.

For the year that began in March, it projected revenue to decrease 10% to Y10.761 trillion and net profit to climb 47% to Y255.00 billion.

Seven & i announced a series of measures last month to boost its shareholder value as it sought to head off a takeover bid by Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard.

The company named its first American chief executive, former Walmart executive Stephen Hayes Dacus, and said it planned to list its North American convenience-store business. The company also agreed to sell a group of superstores and other businesses to U.S. private-equity firm Bain Capital for $5.4 billion and said it would buy back up to $13 billion of its own shares, using proceeds from the sale of the businesses as well as from the initial public offering.

The 7-Eleven owner in September rejected an initial $39 billion buyout bid from Couche-Tard, saying the proposal underestimated the company's value. Couche-Tard later raised its offer to about $47 billion and in January submitted a revised, yen-denominated proposal at Seven & i's request.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: Tietoevry Unit Continues Cloud, Infrastructure Support for Outokumpu
data.description:
Tietoevry's Tietoevry Tech Services received a new contract to continue developing and securing Outokumpu's digital infrastructure.

Under the new five-year deal, the Finnish IT services company will provide critical infrastructure services, including management of SAP platform and Microsoft Azure public cloud services, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: DAX Resumes Decline as Reciprocal Tariffs Take Effect
data.description:
Frankfurt's DAX opened 2.5% lower around 19.790 on Wednesday, as Europe's market sell-off resumed with investors reacting to the overnight rollout of sweeping US tariffs, including a 20% levy on EU imports.

The escalating trade tensions are increasing worries about a potential recession and boosting demand for safe-haven assets.

On the domestic front, talks between the conservatives and the Social Democrats are to resume this morning after long negotiations on Tuesday.

Negotiators said that a coalition agreement is likely to be announced by the afternoon.
